item.title: Version 1.7
item.description:
🆕 Checklist window

A new free-floating Checklist window shows every category, group, and individual destination for the active job with a green check beside anything that already has photos on disk. Designed to be parked on a second monitor or out of the way of the preview so the photographer can glance at "who's left" between groups.

  • Toolbar button next to Tools, or hit ⌘⌥K.
  • Pinned station summary with progress bar stays at the top while you scroll — overall completion is always visible.
  • SAT / SUN tabs when a job has multiple stations.
  • Resizable + draggable like Client Display; remembers position and size across launches.
  • Live updates — every capture debounces a rescan ~500ms later, so a dancer flips to ✓ within a beat of the shutter release.
  • Click a row to drill the sidebar to that destination.

🎯 Thumbnail strip grouped by destination

When you switch between dancers (or any destination folder) during a shoot, the filmstrip now shows the dancer's name above each run of photos and a lime divider between runs that went to different destinations — easier to spot at studio distance which shots belong to whom.

🧩 UI polish across the chrome

  • Camera-properties strip collapsed from two rows to one, reclaiming vertical space for the preview pane. Redundant filename + shot count around the shutter button removed (already shown in the thumbnail strip).
  • Top breadcrumb now shows the full drilled-in path including the selected individual's name — no more stopping at the wrapper folder.
  • Sidebar simplified — duplicated breadcrumb removed (covered by the top strip) and the unused tag-filter row taken out.

No functional changes to camera tethering, capture, or save destinations. All UI-only.

item.title: Version 1.6
item.description:
🆕 Roll Call integration

MMTether can now post each capture to a Roll Call session, so dancers see their photo flip from pending to ✓ on every iPad on the floor in real time — no extra clicks from the photographer.

  • Settings → Capture → Roll Call — opt-in toggle (off by default). When enabled, fill in the session URL + team password from your Roll Call session settings.
  • Dancer name is derived from your folder convention — e.g. …/Class 09/INDIVIDUAL/Harper Ko/IMG_0001.NEF → Harper Ko gets marked done.
  • Group photos are auto-marked too — shooting into a /GROUP/ subfolder flips the class's group photo to ✓.
  • Class + day disambiguation — handles dancers who share a name across classes by reading the class number and day (SAT/SUN) from the folder path.
  • Never blocks capture — fire-and-forget POST with an 8-second timeout. If Roll Call is down, slow, or misconfigured, captures keep saving locally with zero delay.

🐛 Capture-destination respawn fix

Fixed a long-standing bug where photos could silently start saving to ~/Pictures/SHUTR LAB instead of your chosen destination folder. The helper subprocess resets its capture directory to its built-in default on every respawn (watchdog kill, shutdown event, manual disconnect/reconnect), but the app wasn't re-applying your active destination after the reconnect. Now it does — the destination + filename template are cached and re-pushed inside the connect-success path, so any helper recovery preserves where your photos actually land.

This affected anyone who shot through a power-cycle, USB reseat, or any event that triggered a helper respawn during a session. From v1.6 forward, the destination follows the helper through the respawn invisibly.

✨ Visual polish

  • Top-bar wordmark nudged to its final optical position.

No behavior changes if you don't enable Roll Call. Existing tether workflows are unchanged.

item.title: Version 1.5
item.description:
Small visual polish: the SHUTR LAB wordmark in the top toolbar is now optically centered against the chips and buttons beside it.

The underlying SVG had uneven empty space above and below the artwork — about 21 SVG units of padding on top and 40 on the bottom — so when SwiftUI centered the image frame, the visible mark drifted upward. v1.5 corrects the asset's viewBox origin so the mark sits at the bar's geometric center.

No functional changes from v1.4. If you weren't bothered by the alignment, you'll see nothing different.

item.title: Version 1.4
item.description:
Hotfix for the post-shutdown reconnect loop on macOS Sequoia 15.7.x.

v1.3's hijack guard handled the SD-card-on-launch case. A small number of users on Sequoia 15.7.2 reported a different failure: after the camera body fired its auto-power-off shutdown event mid-session, every subsequent reconnect either saw zero cameras (even after killing ptpcamerad) or briefly enumerated the camera but hung in EdsOpenSession until the helper watchdog killed the process. The loop continued indefinitely.

Diagnosis: when the helper was force-killed during an active EDSDK session, Sequoia 15.7.x left the kernel-side IOUSB claim wedged for many seconds. The next helper saw a device that "exists" but couldn't be opened. Newer Sequoia point releases release the claim faster and don't show the symptom.

v1.4 ships the recovery: on the camera-shutdown event, the helper now gracefully closes the EDSDK session before the bridge tears it down — so the kernel sees a clean USB release first. The mid-session daemon-kill fallback also now handles imagecaptureext alongside ptpcamerad and waits longer for launchd respawn. New diagnostic logging captures helper state on every connect attempt for faster remote debugging.

If you weren't seeing the loop before, you'll see no behavioral change.

item.title: Version 1.3
item.description:
Reliable connect with SD card inserted, even after camera shutdown / sleep / unplug.

v1.2 mitigated the "ptpcamerad claims the camera before EDSDK can" bug by killing the daemon when EDSDK couldn't see the device. Real-user logs proved that wasn't enough — the daemon respawns within ~500 ms via launchd and reclaims the PTP session before EdsOpenSession can complete, so the session-open call hung for 21+ seconds and the bridge watchdog killed the helper before recovery could fire.

v1.3 ships the actual fix: at app launch, SHUTR LAB now writes defaults -currentHost write com.apple.ImageCapture disableHotPlug -bool YES so macOS no longer auto-grabs hot-plugged Canon bodies. The preference persists across launches and reboots. Combined with a one-time killall ptpcamerad imagecaptureext to drop any current claim, EDSDK gets a clean window for both enumeration and session-open from the very first attempt — no more "remove the SD card to reconnect" workaround.

Trade-off: macOS auto-import on plug is also disabled for iPhones / other PTP devices on this Mac. Reversible at any time via Image Capture's lower-left preference, or:

defaults -currentHost write com.apple.ImageCapture disableHotPlug -bool NO

The in-helper killall fallback from v1.2 stays as defense-in-depth.

item.title: Version 1.2
item.description:
Hotfix: camera with SD card full of photos now connects reliably without removing the card.

  • Recovers automatically when macOS's ptpcamerad daemon hijacks the USB session. When an SD card with photos is inserted, the system's photo-import daemon claims the camera before EDSDK can — symptoms were a stuck "transfer files mode" icon on the body and the app spinning forever on connect. The fix detects this state (either EdsGetCameraList returning OK with count=0 across all retries, or EdsOpenSession returning DEVICE_BUSY indefinitely), kills the daemon, waits 1.5 s for it to respawn, and retries. Daemon is launchd-managed and respawns automatically — no other apps are affected.
  • Both connect failure modes covered. The fix runs once during initial enumeration and again during session-open, so whether the daemon's blocking the device list or just the PTP session, the camera comes through.
  • Pulling the SD card is no longer a workaround you have to remember.

Includes everything from 0.1.12: the folder-panel overhaul, group picker with per-station filtering, folder tagging, tag-filtered sidebar, write-queue ring on shutter, resizable sidebar, and inline + Group / + Folder add affordances.

item.title: Version 0.1.12
item.description:
Big folder-panel overhaul focused on real recital-scale jobs (80+ groups, mixed structures, hundreds of dancers).

Folder browsing

  • Searchable group picker. The wall of group chips is gone — click the picker, type to filter, hit Return. Type "lyrical aa" to jump to Lyrical AA - Dancing Queen, no more scrolling 80 chips in a narrow column.
  • Per-station filtering. The picker now only lists groups that actually exist on disk inside the active station. On Hanford you see Hanford's 24 groups, not the union of every station's 120.
  • FOLDERS HERE shows whatever's on disk at any drill level. The old strict GROUP+INDIVIDUALS pattern dead-ended folders that didn't match (singular INDIVIDUAL, dancer subfolders, etc.). Now the sidebar browses the actual filesystem at any depth.
  • Path breadcrumb pinned at the top. A new strip under the SHUTR LAB wordmark shows job › station › group › subject. Last segment is accent so the destination reads at studio distance. Click any earlier segment to drill back.
  • Resizable sidebar. Drag the seam between the sidebar and the main pane to widen or narrow it. Persisted across launches.

Folder tagging

  • Right-click any folder chip → tag with a macOS Finder color. Tags use the standard xattr system, so they show up in Finder's own tag sidebar / sort order. Tag a dancer green when she's done shooting.
  • Tag-filtered sidebar. A row of color dots above the chip list. Click red to hide every chip that's not red-tagged. Click again or click ALL to clear. Active chips stay visible regardless of filter so you don't lose your place.

Capture-day quality-of-life

  • Delete from Disk. Right-click any thumbnail in the filmstrip → Delete from Disk… → confirm. Permanent delete with a Lightroom-style irreversible warning.
  • Write-queue ring on the shutter. Thin lime arc fills as captures pile up and drains as files land on disk. Shifts to amber after 3 s of stalled writes — surfaces a wedged helper or full disk before you keep mashing the shutter.
  • + Group / + Folder inline add affordances. Drop the Edit Categories sheet entirely; create groups and dancers right where you'd select them. Validates illegal characters (/, :) and duplicate names instead of silently failing.
  • Open Folder sheet for an existing job no longer pushes Open as Job offscreen on big rosters — body scrolls, footer stays pinned.

Naming. What used to be called "Categories" is now called "Groups" everywhere it's user-visible — closer to the dance-studio language of "the dance group's routine."

Under the hood. 24 unit tests now cover the path-parsing and folder-creation logic, the same layer where every recent sidebar regression has lived. Runs via xcodebuild test before each release.

item.title: Version 0.1.11
item.description:
Diagnostic + entitlement fixes for the EOS R5 C connection issue. Targeted at users whose R5 C connects to EOS Utility but hangs in SHUTR LAB.

  • Helper subprocess now ships with its own entitlements file. Until now MMTetherHelper was signed with zero entitlements while the main app had USB / library-validation entitlements. On macOS Sequoia, this mismatch can silently block EdsOpenSession on Cinema EOS bodies even after the main app has been granted Camera and USB access. The helper now mirrors the relevant subset: cs.disable-library-validation, device.usb, cs.allow-dyld-environment-variables.
  • Step-by-step EDSDK call tracing in the helper log. Every EdsGetCameraList / EdsGetChildAtIndex / EdsOpenSession call now logs entry, exit, and a named return code. A 20-second gap between two timestamps unambiguously identifies which call hung.
  • Millisecond timestamps on every helper log line. Hangs were previously invisible because all the lines looked the same; now [helper 14:35:42.103] tells us cadence at a glance.
  • Helper start/stop boundaries are logged with PID — restart cadence (the bridge watchdog kills the helper after 20s of silence) is now obvious in the log.

item.title: Version 0.1.10
item.description:
Fixes intermittent connection failures on the EOS R5 C and other Cinema EOS bodies.

  • EdsOpenSession now retries on DEVICE_BUSY (up to 12 attempts × 500 ms = ~6 seconds of patience). The R5 C in particular returns BUSY for several seconds after USB enumeration while its photo-mode firmware finishes booting — the camera is "alive" to ImageCaptureCore well before it's actually ready to accept a PTP session-open. Older R5 / R6 / R3 bodies open on the first attempt, so this costs them nothing.
  • UILock around the tethering setup. Canon's own EDSDK sample brackets bulk property writes with kEdsCameraStatusCommand_UILock / UIUnLock so the camera body's UI thread stops fighting our PTP writes. Without it, SetSaveTo and SetCapacity can silently fail on Cinema EOS bodies whose menu system has more state to race against.
  • Diagnostic log shortcut. The connection-error screen now has a "Show Diagnostic Log" button that reveals ~/Library/Logs/SHUTR-Lab/helper.log in Finder — drag it into a support email and the EDSDK call/response chain is right there.
  • Human-readable EDSDK errors in the helper log: "DEVICE_BUSY (0x81)" instead of just "0x81". Faster to diagnose unfamiliar codes.

item.title: Version 0.1.9
item.description:
Better support for the Canon EOS R5 C and other Cinema EOS bodies.

  • The R5 C has a hardware Photo / Video switch on the bottom of the body. In Video mode it presents itself as a Cinema EOS device and won\u{2019}t appear over USB to tethering software at all — EDSDK simply can\u{2019}t see it. This release surfaces that caveat in three places so users figure it out faster:
    • Connect-error help text — the \u{201C}Why does this happen?\u{201D} expandable on the connection-error screen now mentions the switch.
    • First-run onboarding — step 1 calls out the R5 C explicitly.
    • Status-badge tooltip — once a Cinema EOS body connects, hovering the status pill shows a reminder about the mode switch.
  • The EOS R5 C profile was already in the supported-cameras catalog (USB ID 0x3303) — this release just makes the workflow gotcha discoverable instead of leaving users guessing why nothing happens.

item.title: Version 0.1.8
item.description:
Stability + a tighter UI. Verified on macOS Sequoia 15.7.

  • Connection-thrash fix. When the camera fired repeated sleep/shutdown events the connect screen could flicker between "Connecting" and "Error" indefinitely. Now there's a 2-second cooldown between attempts and a 3-strike failure cap that surfaces a clear "Power-cycle the body or replug the cable, then click Reconnect" message instead of looping. Replugging or clicking Reconnect always clears the backoff.
  • Slow-wake handshake. Connect watchdog raised from 12s to 20s so a body waking from sleep doesn't get killed mid-EdsOpenSession.
  • Shutter button moved up into the right edge of the top settings strip, between the two rows of camera-property pills. The bottom shutter dock is gone — that's roughly 70-90pt of extra vertical room for the preview and thumbnail strip.
  • View-tools dock compressed from a 150pt-wide 2-column grid to a 78pt-wide single column of seven compact squares (Grid, Flash, Client, Next, Roster, Sheet, Find). Preview pane gains ~70pt of horizontal room.
  • Removed: the capture sound (and its toggle in Settings); the redundant "Refresh" button in the view-tools dock — settings auto-refresh on every property-change event from the body.
  • macOS Sequoia 15: onboarding now mentions the system "Allow new USB accessories to connect" prompt. Cleaned up macOS 15 deprecations (no behavior change).

item.title: Version 0.1.7
item.description:
Built for long studio sessions — 6,000+ shots in an afternoon.

  • Native macOS Finder color tags replace the star rating system. Press 1–7 (or click the colored dots in the shot toolbar) to apply Red / Orange / Yellow / Green / Blue / Purple / Gray. Tags are written directly to each photo file — open Finder after the shoot, sort by color, edit only the tagged ones.
  • One-time migration converts existing star ratings to color tags automatically.
  • ⌘L Quick Subject Switcher: Spotlight-style fuzzy search across every station, subject, group, and member. Built for 100+ groups with hundreds of members.
  • Drill-down navigation in the folder sidebar — tapping a group collapses the rest of the list, surfacing GROUP / INDIVIDUALS / MEMBERS right under the active subject.
  • ⌘N Next Subject: pre-filled sheet, type name, hit Return — folder created and switched.
  • ⇧⌘I Roster Import: paste a list of names (or load CSV), bulk-create the day's subject folders before the first kid walks in.
  • ⇧⌘P Contact Sheet PDF: streaming page generator with cover, configurable grid (3×3 → 6×8), filenames, tag color dots, timestamps, and per-color filtering.
  • ⇧⌘O Open Existing Folder: scan a pre-built folder tree and adopt it as a job — auto-detects stations, categories, and group-style structure.
  • Camera settings consolidated into the top bar (MODE/TV/AV/ISO/±EV on row 1, WB/STYLE/METER/DRIVE/QUALITY on row 2). Side dock is now a compact grid of square view-tool buttons.
  • Battery + card-space chips next to the camera status pill, color-coded thresholds, polled every 30s and after every capture.
  • Thumbnail filmstrip moved to a horizontal dock under the preview, with auto-scroll to the active shot.
  • Shot toolbar with physical buttons for every keyboard shortcut: Prev / Next / Reveal / 7 tag colors / Clear.
  • Capture history limit raised from 200 to 10,000. Thumbnail rendering uses ImageIO downsampling — never loads a full RAW for a strip cell.

item.title: Version 0.1.6
item.description:
• Capture sound and Reveal-on-Capture toggles now actually work — these settings existed in 0.1.5 but were silently dead.
  • Card-only mode preview fixed — switching to Card-only after shooting to Mac no longer leaves a stale Mac shot in the preview pane.
  • Restart Helper button on the connection-error screen, with a "Why does this happen?" explanation of the macOS PTP daemon issue.
  • Grid / Flash / Client View redesigned as distinct pill-style buttons with a "VIEW" section header — visually separates app-side overlays from camera settings.
  • Grid overlay no longer extends past the image into the black letterbox.
  • Client Display window auto-fades the filename badge when opened mid-shoot, and properly cleans up its timer when closed.
  • Thumbnail strip prunes deleted-in-Finder files automatically; double-tap to reveal no longer also fires single-tap selection.
  • Settings: visual-flash toggle and "Show again" button to reopen the first-run setup guide.

item.title: Version 0.1.5
item.description:
• Save target selector in the folder sidebar — Mac, Card, or Both.
  • Filename template now visible in the sidebar with quick token toggles and live preview.
  • Dedicated exposure strip (MODE / TV / AV / ISO / ±EV) above the preview pane.
  • Capture flash on the preview when the shutter fires — confirms a shot landed even with sound off.
  • Composition grid overlay: Off / Rule of Thirds / 4×4. Toggle from the settings panel or with ⌘'.
  • Client Display window (⇧⌘D) — full-bleed view of the latest shot for a second monitor.
  • Capture history scrubbing: ⌘← / ⌘→ steps through past shots; ⌘R reveals the selected shot in Finder.
  • Connection-error banner with one-click Restart Helper / Reconnect actions and a plain-English explanation of the macOS PTP daemon issue.
  • First-run sheet that walks you through the macOS Image Capture preference that prevents the system from grabbing the camera.

item.title: Version 0.1.4
item.description:
• Larger SHUTR LAB wordmark in the session bar.
  • Folder picker promoted to an always-visible left sidebar — faster subject switching during a shoot.

item.title: Version 0.1.3
item.description:
Auto-update test release — verifies the Sparkle pipeline end-to-end.

No functional changes from 0.1.2.

item.title: Version 0.1.2
item.description:
• First Sparkle-enabled release — auto-update flow live.
  • New Check for Updates button at the bottom of the app.
  • Recital folder workflow: stations, editable categories, group/individuals split.
